hypothesis: Migrate 5 mini-games from source repos into portal with localStorage leaderboards
ceo_rationale: 5 games successfully migrated with zero regressions. localStorage leaderboard works well for small-audience portal. Build and lint pass on first attempt; 2 QA iterations for polish.
learned: Canvas games migrate cleanly by preserving game loop logic and stripping auth/API calls; localStorage is sufficient for single-site leaderboards; inlined data deps beat shared modules for single consumers.
anti_patterns: Do not create shared data modules for one-off dependencies (e.g., teams array used by single game); inline instead for clarity

playbook_proposals.content: When migrating Canvas games, preserve game loop logic intact; strip auth/API calls but keep core game mechanics
Use localStorage for leaderboards in small-audience portals; no sync or cross-device state needed for friends leagues
Inline data dependencies when single consumer exists; defer shared data modules until second consumer surfaces
